summary: 22 tests run, 18 passed, 4 failed, 0 errors, 0 skipped. All 4 failures are raw ApiError repr leaking in error messages across get_type, get, update, and delete actions when handling invalid inputs. CRUD lifecycle (create, get, update, delete) works correctly. Connector type reference data is solid. List filtering by component_type and connector_type works well. Data consistency between list counts is correct (connections + operations = all). The get response for deleted connectors does not expose the deleted flag in the response dict (only in raw XML), which is a known Boomi soft-delete pattern also seen in environments (QA-009).

issues.title: manage_connector get_type leaks raw ApiError repr on invalid connector type
issues.description: When get_type is called with an invalid connector_type, the error message contains the raw ApiError repr including 'ApiError(\n    message=...', 'response=<boomi.net.transport.response.Response object at 0x...>'. This exposes internal SDK object references to the user.
issues.expected: Clean error like: "Connector type 'nonexistent-connector-type-xyz' not found. Use action='list_types' to see available types."
issues.root_cause: The except ApiError handler in the get_type action uses str(e) or repr(e) which produces the raw ApiError representation instead of extracting just the message/error_detail fields.

issues.title: manage_connector get leaks raw ApiError repr on invalid component_id
issues.description: When get is called with an invalid component_id, the error message contains the raw ApiError repr including internal SDK object references (response=<...object at 0x...>). The error_detail from Boomi ('ComponentId ... is invalid') is embedded in the ApiError repr rather than extracted cleanly.
issues.expected: Clean error like: "ComponentId fake-connector-id-xyz is invalid. Provide a valid componentId to complete this action."
issues.root_cause: Same pattern as QA-018: except handler uses str(e)/repr(e) on ApiError instead of _extract_api_error_msg() or accessing e.error_detail directly.

issues.title: manage_connector update leaks raw ApiError repr on invalid component_id
issues.description: When update is called with an invalid component_id, the error message contains the raw ApiError repr. Same root cause as QA-019.
issues.expected: Clean error message extracting the error_detail from ApiError
issues.root_cause: Same pattern: except ApiError handler uses str(e)/repr(e) instead of clean message extraction.

issues.title: manage_connector delete leaks raw ApiError repr on invalid component_id
issues.description: When delete is called with an invalid component_id, the error message contains the raw ApiError repr. Same root cause as QA-019 and QA-020.
issues.expected: Clean error message extracting the error_detail from ApiError
issues.root_cause: Same pattern: except ApiError handler uses str(e)/repr(e) instead of clean message extraction.

observations.title: get response does not expose deleted flag for soft-deleted connectors
observations.description: When a connector is soft-deleted via the delete action, the get action still returns _success=true with the full component data. The XML contains deleted='true' but the response dict has deleted: None. This is the same Boomi soft-delete pattern observed for environments (QA-009). The list action correctly filters deleted connectors from default results.

observations.title: list deleted and current_version fields are strings not booleans
observations.description: In list responses, the 'deleted' field is the string 'False' (not boolean false) and 'current_version' is the string 'True'/'False'. This is the same issue as Bug #45 (query_components) and appears to be a systemic issue with ComponentMetadata query results. Not filing a new bug since it's the same root cause.

observations.title: CRUD lifecycle works end-to-end
observations.description: Create -> Get -> Update -> Get (verify) -> Delete lifecycle works correctly for HTTP connectors. The create action returns component_id at top level. The update correctly increments version (1->2). Delete uses metadata_delete method which is reliable.

observations.title: Data consistency between list filters is correct
observations.description: list(all) = 80, list(connection) = 23, list(operation) = 57, sum = 80. show_all=true returns 143 (includes deleted/old versions). Consistent.

observations.title: All 4 bugs share the same root cause
observations.description: All 4 issues (QA-018 through QA-021) are the same pattern: the except ApiError handler in connectors.py uses str(e) or f-string interpolation that produces the raw ApiError repr. The fix is to use _extract_api_error_msg(e) or access e.message/e.error_detail directly, as was done for other tools (e.g., Bug #32 fix for manage_component).

verified_fixes.fix: Added except ApiError branch in get_connector_type using _extract_api_error_msg(e)
verified_fixes.verified: get_type with invalid connector_type returns clean error with list_types hint, no raw ApiError repr

verified_fixes.fix: Added except ApiError branch in get_connector using _extract_api_error_msg(e)
verified_fixes.verified: get with invalid component_id returns clean error including the ID, no raw ApiError repr

verified_fixes.fix: Added except ApiError branch in update_connector using _extract_api_error_msg(e)
verified_fixes.verified: update with invalid component_id returns clean error including the ID, no raw ApiError repr

verified_fixes.fix: Added except ApiError branch in delete_connector using _extract_api_error_msg(e)
verified_fixes.verified: delete with invalid component_id returns clean error including the ID, no raw ApiError repr
